name: apt-utils
title: apt-utils
description:
package management related utility programs
This package contains some less used commandline utilities related
to package management with APT.
.
* apt-ftparchive is used to create Packages and other index files
needed to publish an archive of Debian packages
* apt-sortpkgs is a Packages/Sources file normalizer.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use apt-ftparchive because package management related utility programs.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use apt-ftparchive as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `apt-ftparchive  [-dsq]  [--md5]  [--delink]  [--readonly]  [--contents]  [--arch architecture]` as documented upstream for apt-ftparchive.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `apt-ftparchive packages directory | gzip > Packages.gz` as documented upstream for apt-ftparchive.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair apt-ftparchive with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `apt-ftparchive --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: apt-ftparchive - Utility to generate index files
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install apt. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `apt-ftparchive packages directory | gzip > Packages.gz`. package management related utility programs.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: package management related utility programs.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: apt-ftparchive - Utility to generate index files.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install apt
Install with: sudo apt install apt
